endpoints_called.enrich: NOT called — no enrichment step exists in either script
endpoints_called.note: Both scripts call only the single search endpoint. There is no separate enrich or reveal step.

what_apollo_actually_returns_on_free_tie.name: null — field not returned
what_apollo_actually_returns_on_free_tie.last_name: obfuscated — returned as last_name_obfuscated, actual value hidden
what_apollo_actually_returns_on_free_tie.email: null — has_email=true indicates email exists but value withheld

root_cause: Apollo free/basic tier obfuscates contact data. reveal_personal_emails=True is ignored unless on a paid Apollo plan. There is no free enrichment API that bypasses this.

options.option_1: Upgrade Apollo to a paid plan (starts ~$49/mo for basic email credits) — unlocks email and LinkedIn data
options.option_2: Use Apollo search to get company names, then cross-reference with Hunter.io domain search (find emails by company domain)
options.option_3: Use RocketReach or Clearbit as alternative enrichment APIs
options.option_4: Wait for PDL monthly quota reset (April 24) — PDL returns full contact data including emails and LinkedIn URLs on current plan
options.recommended: Option 2 — Hunter.io domain search costs nothing extra (already subscribed) and can recover emails from company names. Add a post-search enrichment step to both scripts.

proposed_fix.step_1: Apollo search returns company name (e.g. 'SaaS Alerts')
proposed_fix.step_2: Search Hunter.io domain search API for that company to get domain + email pattern
proposed_fix.step_3: Use Hunter.io email finder to construct verified email from first name + domain
proposed_fix.step_4: For LinkedIn: use company name + title in a web search to find the person's profile
proposed_fix.implementation: Add a hunter_enrich(company_name, first_name, last_name) function to both scripts
